page: Amazon Web Services r5d.large is a memory-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The instance provides 2 virtual CPUs mapped to 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

For local storage, the r5d.large includes a bundled 75 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. This local SSD provides low-latency scratch space directly attached to the host. The network configuration features a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates that the r5d.large occupies a lower-tier performance bracket for compute and memory throughput. In stress-ng testing, single-core CPU performance scored 1276.49, while multi-core performance reached 1678.07. Memory bandwidth tests showed 96.24 GB/sec for cached small blocks, dropping to 13.57 GB/sec for uncached large block reads and 11.56 GB/sec for writes. Database operations under Passmark scored 675.15, and Redis SET operations achieved 1.69 million operations per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r5d.large offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio paired with local NVMe storage, making it suitable for workloads requiring more RAM than compute power without the premium of larger instance sizes. Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, this instance is best suited for memory-intensive applications with modest CPU requirements, such as caching layers, small databases, and workloads utilizing local temporary storage.
description: Amazon Web Services r5d.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. Utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core, paired with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a bundled 75 GB NVMe SSD instance store. Network performance is anchored by a 1 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmark evaluations place this instance in lower-tier performance categories, with a single-core Geekbench score of 1055.0 and a multi-core score of 1247.0. Memory bandwidth reaches 96.24 GB/sec for cached small blocks but drops to 13.57 GB/sec for large blocks. Qualitatively, the r5d.large provides a resource-dense memory profile with local storage for workloads that do not demand high compute throughput. It is best suited for small databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications requiring local scratch space.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5d.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a bundled 75 GB NVMe SSD, designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring local scratch storage.

tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, DDR4 memory, and a bundled local NVMe SSD for low-latency scratch storage.

16.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
Bundled 75 GB NVMe SSD local instance storage
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for caching layers, small databases, and memory-intensive tasks
